title: Mutation ledger: file tracking with external revert detection
purpose: Add a mutation ledger to the action log — a derived view that tracks every file modification during the session. The ledger answers "what files did I change?" without relying on git status, which shows pre-existing dirty files and changes from other agents in a shared working tree. External revert detection is lazy: when queried, each file is hashed and compared against the post-mutation hash. No filesystem watching, no polling.
task: Create crates/norn/src/session/mutation_ledger.rs with MutationLedgerEntry (file_path, operation, first/last tool_call_id, revert_status, diff_stats). The MutationLedger is built from ActionLog entries — each time a mutation tool completes, the ledger updates. Add the mutations query to the action_log tool (NTA-002) that returns ledger entries with lazy revert detection. The ledger is a derived view, not a separate store — it reads from ActionLog and the filesystem.

requirements.title: Define MutationLedgerEntry struct
requirements.spec: MutationLedgerEntry SHALL be defined in crates/norn/src/session/mutation_ledger.rs with fields: file_path (PathBuf), operation (MutationOp enum: Created, Modified, Deleted), first_tool_call_id (String — first tool call that touched this file), last_tool_call_id (String — most recent tool call that touched this file), revert_status (RevertStatus enum: Active, ExternallyReverted, SubsequentlyModified), and diff_stats (DiffStats struct: lines_added: u32, lines_removed: u32). MutationLedgerEntry SHALL derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize.
acceptance: crates/norn/src/session/mutation_ledger.rs exists
MutationLedgerEntry has all specified fields
MutationOp has exactly 3 variants: Created, Modified, Deleted
RevertStatus has exactly 3 variants: Active, ExternallyReverted, SubsequentlyModified
DiffStats has lines_added and lines_removed fields
All types derive Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize

requirements.title: MutationLedger derived from ActionLog entries
requirements.spec: MutationLedger SHALL be a struct that builds its entries from the ActionLog. When ActionLog::record_completion is called for a mutation tool (edit, write, apply_patch), MutationLedger::record_mutation SHALL be called with the file path(s), operation, tool_call_id, and diff stats extracted from the tool output. Multiple mutations to the same file update the existing entry (last_tool_call_id, diff_stats accumulate). The ledger is NOT a separate store — it derives from ActionLog data.
acceptance: MutationLedger.record_mutation updates or creates entries
Same file mutated twice: last_tool_call_id updated, first_tool_call_id preserved
Diff stats from subsequent mutations accumulate
Ledger entries retrievable by file_path
Ledger entries are session-scoped (only current session's tool calls)

requirements.title: External revert detection via hash comparison at query time
requirements.spec: When the mutation ledger is queried (not at mutation time), each entry's file SHALL be read and hashed. The hash is compared against the post-mutation hash stored when the last tool call touching that file completed. If the hash differs AND no subsequent tool call in the ActionLog touched that file, the entry's revert_status is set to ExternallyReverted. If a subsequent tool call DID touch the file, revert_status is SubsequentlyModified. If the hash matches, revert_status is Active. Hash computation uses the same algorithm as ExpiryCondition (SHA-256 of file content).
acceptance: File unchanged since mutation: revert_status = Active
File changed externally (no subsequent tool call): revert_status = ExternallyReverted
File changed by a later tool call: revert_status = SubsequentlyModified
File deleted since mutation: revert_status = ExternallyReverted
Hash comparison only happens at query time, not at mutation time
No filesystem watching or polling

requirements.title: Store post-mutation content hash on record_mutation
requirements.spec: When record_mutation is called, the MutationLedger SHALL compute and store the content hash of the file as it exists after the mutation. For deletions, store a sentinel hash. This stored hash is the baseline for external revert detection at query time.
acceptance: record_mutation computes SHA-256 hash of file content after mutation
Hash stored on the MutationLedgerEntry
Deleted files store a sentinel hash value
Hash computation does not block the tool dispatch path (file is already on disk)

requirements.title: Add mutations query to the action_log tool
requirements.spec: The action_log tool (NTA-002) SHALL accept query="mutations". The mutations query returns all MutationLedgerEntry values with revert detection evaluated at query time. The filter object's file field filters to entries matching that file path. Output structure: {"query": "mutations", "count": N, "entries": [{file_path, operation, first_tool_call_id, last_tool_call_id, revert_status, diff_stats}, ...]}.
acceptance: action_log tool accepts query="mutations"
mutations query returns MutationLedgerEntry values with revert_status evaluated at query time
filter.file limits results to matching file path
Output has query, count, entries fields
Each entry includes all MutationLedgerEntry fields including revert_status

requirements.title: Tests for mutation ledger
requirements.spec: Tests SHALL verify: (1) single file mutation creates entry, (2) multiple mutations to same file update last_tool_call_id, (3) external revert detected (modify file after mutation, query shows ExternallyReverted), (4) subsequent tool mutation shows SubsequentlyModified, (5) unchanged file shows Active, (6) deleted file shows ExternallyReverted, (7) mutations query through action_log tool returns correct entries, (8) session-scoped — only current session mutations shown.
acceptance: Test: single mutation creates entry with correct fields
Test: two mutations to same file updates last_tool_call_id
Test: external file modification detected as ExternallyReverted
Test: subsequent tool mutation detected as SubsequentlyModified
Test: unchanged file detected as Active
Test: mutations query returns correct count and entries
All tests pass with cargo test -p norn

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement cross-session mutation tracking — the ledger is session-scoped (NG1)
SHALL NOT implement real-time file watching or polling — detection is lazy at query time (CO2)
SHALL NOT integrate with git status — the ledger is independent of git
SHALL NOT implement follow-up action storage — that is NTA-004
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings produces zero errors
cargo fmt -p norn -- --check produces no diff
cargo test -p norn passes all tests
action_log tool accepts query="mutations" and returns correct entries
